tasks.description: Create Invoica MCP Server — tools for create_invoice, list_invoices, check_settlement
tasks.context:
Create a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server at backend/src/mcp/invoica-mcp-server.ts that exposes Invoica as an MCP tool provider.

MCP servers let Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, and other AI assistants call Invoica directly. This is viral developer distribution.

WHAT TO BUILD:
A standalone MCP server (runs as a separate process) using the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk package.

FILE: backend/src/mcp/invoica-mcp-server.ts (max 200 lines)

TOOLS TO EXPOSE:
1. create_invoice — creates an invoice via Invoica API
   Input: { amount: number, currency: string, customerEmail: string, customerName: string, apiKey: string }
   Output: { invoiceId, paymentUrl, amount, status }

2. list_invoices — lists recent invoices for an API key
   Input: { apiKey: string, limit?: number }
   Output: array of invoice summaries

3. check_settlement — checks if an invoice has been settled on-chain
   Input: { invoiceId: string, apiKey: string }
   Output: { settled: boolean, txHash?, chain?, amount? }

IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN:
- Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk Server class with StdioServerTransport
- Each tool calls the Invoica REST API at process.env.INVOICA_API_URL (default: https://api.invoica.ai)
- Use native fetch for HTTP calls (no extra dependencies)
- Handle errors gracefully — return error message as tool result, not throw

PACKAGE ADDITIONS needed in package.json:
- @modelcontextprotocol/sdk: ^1.0.0

Also create backend/src/mcp/README.md (max 30 lines) with:
- One-line install instruction: npx -y @invoica/mcp
- Claude Desktop config JSON snippet (mcpServers block)
- Cursor config snippet
- The 3 available tools listed briefly

tasks.description: Add MCP server to ecosystem.config.js and package.json bin entry
tasks.context:
After MCP-001 creates the MCP server file, wire it up for distribution.

1. Add to ecosystem.config.js a new PM2 entry for local dev testing:
{
  name: invoica-mcp,
  script: ./backend/src/mcp/invoica-mcp-server.ts,
  interpreter: node,
  interpreter_args: -r ts-node/register,
  cwd: /home/invoica/apps/Invoica,
  autorestart: false,
  env: { TS_NODE_TRANSPILE_ONLY: true, INVOICA_API_URL: https://api.invoica.ai }
}

2. In package.json, add to bin:
{ invoica-mcp: ./backend/src/mcp/invoica-mcp-server.ts }

This enables: npx invoica-mcp as the install command for users.
